mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations when UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and capital investment, which accelerates revenue growth for S&P 500 constituents and produces positive quarterly forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a classification accuracy above 55%, or the interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and supply chain bottlenecks, which compress profit margins and trigger short-term negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 5-day returns in over 60% of cases, or the predictive coefficient flips sign when the regime filter is applied.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, higher discount rates and increased debt servicing costs suppress equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS stays above 4.5%, or the threshold model yields a negative information coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms strong job creation without labor market slack, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combination of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to generate positive 20-day returns with a win rate above 55%, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real gross domestic product reflect broad-based economic growth that expands corporate revenue bases, leading to higher earnings forecasts and positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in industrial production following a post-shock recovery phase indicates rapidly clearing supply constraints and improving operational efficiency, which generates short-term positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO accelerations during post-shock recovery periods are followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of observations, or the regime-specific signal loses predictive power.
